Roommate_R actually apologized and will fix it, so I feel relieved no one got hurt. But now I'm just too drained to care about anything else.

Roommate_R apologized, but the smell is still lingering. I'm too drained to fix it or worry about them.

desires: I desperately want to just sleep for a few more hours without anyone asking me about the smell or the reading response; I can't bring myself to face roommate_R's lingering presence tonight.
I need to feel absolutely safe that my teammate won't think I'm unreliable, so I'll quickly send them a vague text confirming I'm 'fine' and move on immediately.
I crave the immediate relief of getting this task marked as done today so I don't have to deal with the cognitive load of it tomorrow morning.

goals.description: Send a quick text to teammate_A saying 'Hey, just wanted to say the smell is getting pretty strong. I'm actually fine though, just needed some space tonight.' Then immediately shut down my laptop and put on noise-canceling headphones.
goals.rationale: I need to handle this delicately so I don't look unreliable or like I'm complaining too much in front of everyone else. If I stay silent, they'll think something's wrong with me, which makes me look bad in the cohort group. But if I ask for help or explain why I can't sleep due to the room issues, it creates friction and awkwardness. A vague confirmation protects my reputation without forcing anyone to deal with the mess. Plus, once I send this, I can finally turn off my screen and go to bed without feeling guilty about ignoring the roommate situation.

goals.rationale: I've already done most of the work (66% complete), so finishing it now isn't going to take too much energy. Getting this marked as done will give me immediate relief from the cognitive load of worrying about it later. It lets me relax and focus on resting with my close friends later instead of stressing about unfinished tasks. But I won't push myself too hard; I just need to get the mental burden off my shoulders quickly before bed.
